title: tricholepidium maculosum var. subnormale
intro: Tricholepidium maculosum var. subnormale, also known as Kun chai or Guduchi, is a plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family and is native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, improving immune function, and supporting liver health. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Tricholepidium maculosum var. subnormale plant are often made into teas, decoctions, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
